repo_prior_art.cite: pid_gpn_continuous_rho: fixed-rho continuous GPN inverse-threshold proposal for a different event, not a Spearman-band Pr(Y1>Y0) closure envelope

published_prior_art.cite: FanPark2009DTEConfidenceSets treats known dependence-measure DTE bounds; FanPark2010DTEBounds treats unrestricted Makarov bounds; Kellerer1984Duality and BartlKupperLuxPapapantoleon2022Dependence supply the close transport-duality substrate

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_gpn_continuous_rho: rank-copula sharpness/pasting for GPN, not no-gap Spearman-band transport duality for the strict benefit indicator

published_prior_art.cite: Kellerer1984Duality and BartlKupperLuxPapapantoleon2022Dependence are closest; neither states the causal continuous-outcome strict-benefit endpoint over a conditional Spearman band

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_gpn_continuous_rho: inverse rho-star certification for GPN, not an outer-envelope majority-benefit diagnostic

published_prior_art.cite: FirpoRidder2019Functionals motivates direct bounds for DTE functionals but does not give a Spearman-band majority-sign envelope

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_bound_intersection_inference: failed generic endpoint-inference scaffold, not a finite-cell quantile-grid Spearman endpoint estimator

published_prior_art.cite: FanPark2010DTEBounds gives plug-in inference for Makarov endpoints; HuangFangHanleyRosenblum2019FractionBenefit gives finite-support fraction-benefit inference, not continuous Spearman-rank-moment transport endpoints

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_gpn_continuous_rho has inverse-threshold CI fragments, not a directional endpoint law for Spearman-band transport bounds

published_prior_art.cite: ImbensManski2004PartialCI and Stoye2009MoreCI require endpoint limit theory as input; FanPark2010DTEBounds supplies the closest m-out-of-n endpoint-bootstrap precedent

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Theorem 1
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Endpoint attainment, measurable optimizing selectors, and primal-dual equality are not claimed.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Theorem 1 is bound to the closure-sharp integrated feasible value set plus weak-duality outer bounds; no-gap equality is only Conjecture 1.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Theorem 2
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Envelope straddling of 1/2 is not claimed to construct feasible laws on both sides of the majority boundary.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Theorem 2 is accepted only as an outer-envelope diagnostic: certification outside the envelope, indeterminacy at the outer-envelope level when it straddles.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Conjecture 2
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Endpoint inference is conditional on stable dual faces and the stated root-n local sieve-remainder condition.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The bootstrap endpoint-law claim is a conditional field-tier inference conjecture, not an unconditional endpoint theorem.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add a one-line location anchor to BartlKupperLuxPapapantoleon2022Dependence or Kellerer1984Duality naming the exact duality theorem that Conjecture 1 must adapt or bypass.
Add a small two- or three-point rank-grid calculation of one Spearman-band endpoint to make the focal transport object auditable before Stage 0.
For Conjecture 2, state the candidate directional derivative or active-dual-face formula so the derivation target is a concrete limit object rather than only bootstrap consistency.

recommended_next_step: Advance to Stage 0 on the corrected field-tier closure-envelope proposal; prove Conjecture 1 as the real open kernel and keep the scope caveats bound.
